sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old male, presented to the office with a one-week history of right knee pain after a trampoline incident.
He reports hearing a snap and experiencing instant pain on the outside of his knee.
He has not taken any medication for the pain, except for Tylenol, which did not provide relief.
He has been elevating the knee and has not attempted any other treatments.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension, for which he is taking 20 mg of lisinopril most days.
He has not been checking his blood pressure regularly and does not have a blood pressure cuff.
He also has diabetes, for which he is taking metformin 500 mg, and checks his blood sugar levels 4-5 times a week.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his blood sugars have been slightly better, but he does not have a specific diet or exercise plan in place.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20 mg (most days)
Metformin 500 mg
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 180/80, which is higher than the desired range.
The patient's heart rate and oxygen saturation were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight limp and ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect of his knee.
He has a decent range of motion, but experiences pain with movement.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain in his right knee and hypertension that is not well-controlled, as well as uncontrolled diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; lateral ligament strain; hypertension; suspected uncontrolled diabetes; suspected lipid disorder.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 mg daily for pain and swelling in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20 mg and order an echocardiogram to assess the patient's cardiac function and determine if further medication modification is necessary.
I also ordered an A1C and lipid panel to assess the patient's diabetes and lipid profile.
